about.description:
Game Developer since 2022, specializing in gameplay systems, playable ads, and mobile game development. Shipped multiple titles across puzzle, platformer, rhythm, and endless runner genres.
Currently at Amanotes, building scalable gameplay systems for music games.
about.quote: Code is temporary, gameplay is forever.

highlights.title: Cat Dash: Cute Cat Music Game
highlights.desc: A 2D rhythm platformer where every move syncs with the music.
highlights.detail:
A fast-paced 2D rhythm platformer where a reckless cat dashes, jumps, and wall-slides through tight obstacle courses, with every action locked to the beat.

Role: Solo Playable Developer
Built a beat-synced gameplay framework that drives obstacles, traps, and camera feedback directly from the music timeline. Implemented lightweight audio analysis and beat-marking tools for level design, plus responsive dash/jump controls with coyote time and input buffering for a tight, game-feel-first experience. Optimized object pooling and sprite batching to maintain a stable 60fps target on mobile devices.

Created as a polished portfolio project to showcase rhythm-based gameplay design, technical implementation of music sync, and mobile-ready performance

impacts.label: CPI Reduction
impacts.desc: Reduced CPI by 70%, bringing the game from near-dead status to close to passing soft launch — still actively running and iterating

impacts.label: Hypothesis-Driven Testing
impacts.desc: Tested multiple hypotheses not present in the original product, achieving strong results — higher impressions, increased playable completion rates through new effects, redesigned maps with reduced difficulty, and better player feedback

impacts.label: Wordless Tutorial Design
impacts.desc: Designed tutorial maps that teach players within the first 5–10 seconds without any text, images, or explicit instructions

impacts.label: Skill Illusion Moments
impacts.desc: Created gameplay situations that make players feel they won through their own skill and ability, boosting engagement and satisfaction

impacts.label: Portrait Mode Pivot
impacts.desc: Tested hypothesis of switching from the original game's landscape orientation to portrait mode — results were strong enough that the product team published an additional portrait version of the game

hypotheses.name: New visual effects & map redesign
hypotheses.desc: Added new effects and redesigned maps with reduced difficulty compared to the original game, plus better player feedback
hypotheses.result: Higher impressions, increased playable completion rate

hypotheses.name: Portrait mode (landscape → portrait)
hypotheses.desc: Tested switching from the original game's landscape orientation to portrait mode for playable ads
hypotheses.result: Strong performance — product team published an additional portrait version of the game

hypotheses.name: Wordless tutorial in first 5–10s
hypotheses.desc: Designed tutorial maps that teach players purely through level design, no text or images needed
hypotheses.result: Players understood mechanics quickly, improved early retention

highlights.title: Magic Dance Star: Rhythm Tiles
highlights.desc: The company's first playable ad combining rhythm gameplay with 3D Audition-style dance mechanics.
highlights.detail:
The company's first playable ad combining rhythm gameplay with 3D Audition-style dance mechanics. Features polished visual effects and audio feedback that recreate the feel of the original game, with multiple 3D character models optimized to keep the total build size under 5MB.

Role: Solo Playable Developer
Currently in soft launch phase.

impacts.label: First 3D Rhythm Playable
impacts.desc: Built the company's first playable ad combining rhythm mechanics with 3D Audition-style dance gameplay

impacts.label: Visual & Audio Fidelity
impacts.desc: Rich visual effects and audio feedback that recreate the feel of the original game within a playable ad

impacts.label: 3D Model Optimization
impacts.desc: Multiple 3D character models optimized to keep total build size under 5MB

projects.title: Tile Hope: Music Ball Clash
projects.detail:
A fast-paced 3D rhythm music game where you drag to guide a bouncing ball across glowing tiles, chaining hops to the beat while avoiding gaps and off-path falls.

Role: Solo Playable Developer
Implemented beat-synced tile spawning based on BPM and precomputed beat maps, with dynamic patterns, speed scaling, and visual effects all driven by the music timeline. Built responsive drag controls, camera follow, and assist features (auto-aim tolerance, coyote time) to keep the game feel tight even at high speeds. Optimized object pooling, mesh batching, and lightweight shaders to keep memory usage low and maintain a smooth 60fps on a wide range of Android devices, even with longer tracks.

Created as a portfolio project to showcase rhythm-game design, audio-driven level generation, and mobile-ready performance optimization..

projects.title: Dancing Road: Color Ball Run
projects.desc: A rhythm-based color-matching runner where you swipe to guide a ball along a music-driven track, hitting same-color balls to the beat.
projects.detail:
A 3D rhythm-based color ball runner where you swipe to steer along a winding track, switching colors and colliding with same-color balls while dodging mismatched ones, all tightly synced to the music.

Role: Playable Developer
Implemented a beat-driven track system that controls obstacle layout, color-change segments, and collectible patterns based on BPM and predefined beatmaps. Designed the color-state logic so collisions, VFX, and score feedback all react instantly to the current ball color and hit accuracy. Tuned swipe controls with smoothing and assist zones for mobile, and optimized object pooling, material usage, and LOD to keep performance stable on a wide range of Android devices.

Built as a portfolio project to showcase rhythm-runner design, audio-synced level logic, and scalable color-matching mechanics for mobile.

projects.title: Magic Tile 3: Piano Music Game
projects.detail:
A fast-paced piano tiles rhythm game where you tap and hold black tiles while avoiding empty spaces, chaining notes to the beat to complete each song with high accuracy and combos.

Role: Playable Developer
Implemented a timing-accurate input system with configurable hit windows (perfect/great/late/miss), combo and multiplier logic, and real-time score calculation. Built a song/level system that maps audio tracks to tile patterns, supports variable speeds and difficulty, and compensates for audio latency on different devices. Added visual and haptic feedback for hits and misses to keep the experience responsive and satisfying. Optimized tile pooling, UI rendering, and audio loading to maintain a smooth 60fps and short loading times on mobile.

Created as a portfolio project to demonstrate rhythm-game mechanics, audio–input synchronization, and mobile performance optimization for popular piano music gameplay.

projects.title: Duet Cats: Cute Music Game
projects.detail:
A minimalist rhythm-based arcade game inspired by duet-style mechanics, where two linked cats orbit around a center point and you rotate them to weave through incoming obstacles, all tightly synced to the soundtrack.

Role: Playable Developer
Built a twin-orbit control system with precise angular movement, collision detection, and easing curves to keep rotations responsive yet readable at high speeds. Implemented beat-aligned obstacle spawning driven by predefined patterns and BPM data, with dynamic difficulty scaling based on player performance. Tuned camera shake, trails, and hit/miss feedback to reinforce the musical rhythm and maintain a satisfying game feel. Optimized pooling and lightweight shaders to keep performance smooth on mid- to low-end mobile devices.

Created as a portfolio project to showcase minimalist game design, rhythm-synced obstacle patterns, and polished duet-style controls tailored for mobile.

projects.title: Mega Market Idle
projects.desc: An idle shop-management mini game where you stock shelves, hire helpers, and watch revenue grow even while you’re not actively tapping.
projects.detail:
A compact idle-management game built around running a tiny storefront that gradually scales into a bustling shop, balancing upgrades, automation, and satisfying number growth.

Role: Playable Developer
Implemented tap and drag interactions for stocking shelves and upgrading product slots, with clear visual states for locked, active, and maxed-out items. Built a time-based production and revenue system driven by scriptable config data, allowing each item line to define its own base income, production time, and upgrade curve. Added soft-prestige mechanics through global multipliers and manager boosts so short sessions still feel meaningfully rewarding.

Designed upgrade pacing using exponential and sub-exponential growth curves to keep early-game progress snappy while preventing late-game values from exploding uncontrollably. Integrated auto-collect helpers, progress bars, and floating gain text to make passive income feel tangible even when the player is mostly watching. Tuned particles, SFX hooks, and subtle punch/scale animations on key actions (buy, upgrade, collect) to keep the experience tactile and readable on mobile.

Created as a portfolio project to showcase idle-economy balancing, data-driven upgrade systems, and clear, satisfying management UI tailored for short playable-ad length sessions.

projects.title: Decor Room: Animal Music Party
projects.desc: A cozy music-decor game where you design an animal party room, placing interactive items that react and groove to the music.
projects.detail:
A cozy music-decor experience where you design a party room for cute animal characters, placing furniture, lights, and interactive music objects that react to the beat. As you decorate, each item adds sound, animation, or visual effects, turning the room into a living music party scene.

Role: Solo Playable Developer
Built a modular decor system where each object can attach its own sound layer, animation, and VFX, all driven by a shared music timeline. Implemented snap-based placement, drag-and-drop controls, and stateful items (on/off, upgraded variants) to keep decorating simple but expressive on mobile. Created a lightweight audio layering system so multiple objects can add loops and sound effects without overloading the mix, and synchronized key animations (lights, dancing animals, confetti) with musical beats and sections. Optimized batching, pooling, and culling for a stable 60fps target even in heavily decorated rooms.

Created as a portfolio project to highlight casual game UX, audio-reactive systems, and mobile-friendly performance for interactive music scenes.

projects.title: Word Music Explored
projects.desc: AA word puzzle rhythm game where you form words to unlock music layers and explore dynamic soundscapes.
projects.detail:
A hybrid word puzzle and music exploration game where you spell words from given letters to unlock new instruments, loops, and sound layers, gradually building a full track as you progress through each level.

Role: Solo Playable Developer
Designed and implemented a flexible word-generation and validation system, combined with a music layering pipeline that maps player progress to dynamic changes in the soundtrack (adding instruments, filters, and transitions). Built level structures with themed word sets and difficulty curves, plus progression systems that reward longer words and smart letter usage. Synced visual feedback (waves, pulses, particle effects) to both correct words and musical changes to keep the experience readable and satisfying on mobile.

Developed as a portfolio project to showcase hybrid game design (puzzle + music), audio-reactive systems, and a scalable content pipeline for word-based music experiences.

projects.title: Word Music Explored
projects.desc: AA word puzzle rhythm game where you form words to unlock music layers and explore dynamic soundscapes.
projects.detail:
A rhythm-based tap gallery experience where you tap notes to the beat to reveal animated photo walls, stickers, and special visual moments synced with the music.

Role: Solo Playable Developer
Implemented a beatmap-driven tap system that synchronizes note patterns, combo effects, and gallery reveals directly to each song’s timeline. Built a gallery framework where photos, stickers, and overlays can be arranged in themed layouts and unlocked progressively based on player performance. Added lightweight shaders, transitions, and particle effects to highlight key beats while keeping performance smooth on mobile devices.

Developed as a portfolio project to showcase rhythm-based interaction design, music-synced visuals, and stylish gallery presentation for mobile.

projects.title: Cat Bus Jam: Seat Out
projects.desc: A cute traffic puzzle where you match cats to their color-coded buses and clear traffic jams with simple, satisfying moves.
projects.detail:
A charming traffic puzzle game where you slide and rearrange cats and buses on a crowded grid, matching each cat to the bus of the same color while untangling increasingly tricky jams at the station.

Role: Unity Developer
Designed and implemented core color-matching and traffic-unblocking mechanics, with level progression that gradually introduces tighter layouts and more complex jams. Focused on readable UX for drag/tap interactions, clear visual feedback when cats reach the right bus, and smooth animations that reinforce the “cute chaos” theme. Optimized level data and assets for quick loads and stable performance on low- to mid-range Android devices.

Released on Google Play as a casual puzzle title to showcase accessible level design, satisfying match-and-clear gameplay, and mobile-ready performance for a broad audience.

projects.title: Pizza Mania: Slice Sort
projects.desc: A cozy food-themed puzzle where you sort colorful pizza slices onto matching plates to complete perfect pies.
projects.detail:
A casual slice-sorting puzzle game where you drag and stack pizza slices onto matching plates, planning each move to group identical toppings and complete full pizzas with the fewest moves possible. Levels gradually introduce more toppings, plates, and trickier layouts, turning a simple mechanic into a satisfying, brain-teasing experience.

Role: Solo Developer
Designed and implemented core slice-sorting mechanics with drag-and-drop interactions, limited-move challenges, and undo/hint systems to keep the game accessible while rewarding smart planning. Built a flexible level system supporting handcrafted and pattern-based layouts, plus progressive difficulty tuning. Optimized object pooling, sprite atlases, and lightweight UI transitions to maintain smooth performance and quick level reloads on a wide range of Android devices.

Created as a portfolio project to showcase mobile puzzle design, tactile drag-based interactions, and efficient 2D performance optimization in a casual, food-themed setting.

timeline.desc: Building playable ads for music games. Config-driven production, optimization, cross-network deployment.
Developed and published multiple mobile games across puzzle, platformer genres.
